specific_change: Reconfigure the ALB target group (app05-tg) load-balancing algorithm from round_robin to least_outstanding_requests. No capacity change is needed — the existing 8 × c5.xlarge fleet is sufficient. In Terraform, set `load_balancing_algorithm_type = "least_outstanding_requests"` on the `aws_lb_target_group.main` resource.

conclusion.headline: Switch ALB from round_robin to least_outstanding_requests to fix instance imbalance

telemetry_observations: Cluster CPU p95 is 90.1% (p50) reaching 94.0% (p95), indicating hot instances near saturation.
Application p95 latency median is 277ms with p95 at 334ms, breaching the 300ms SLA target.
CPU p50-to-p95 spread is ~10 percentage points (81.1 vs 90.1), signaling uneven distribution.
Memory p95 is moderate at 60.2% (p50), confirming this is not a memory-bound problem.
Network I/O stddev is ~21 Mbps, consistent with uneven request routing across instances.
infrastructure_context: ALB target group (app05-tg) uses load_balancing_algorithm_type = round_robin.
Fleet is 8 × c5.xlarge with no auto-scaling policy configured.
Per-instance breakdown shows a clear 50/50 hot/cold split across the fleet.
correlation_observations: Instances i-001 through i-004 sustain 78-88% CPU (hot) while i-005 through i-008 sustain 8-18% CPU (cold), a ~70 percentage-point variance caused by round-robin ignoring request duration.

reasoning: The per-instance breakdown reveals a stark bimodal CPU distribution: instances i-001 through i-004 run at 78-88% while i-005 through i-008 idle at 8-18%. This pattern is characteristic of round-robin routing with variable request durations — some instances accumulate long-running requests while others finish quickly and sit idle. The cluster-level p95 latency of 334ms breaches the 300ms SLA because the hot instances are saturated. Switching to least_outstanding_requests will route new connections to the instance with the fewest in-flight requests, naturally balancing load regardless of request duration. Adding capacity would not fix the root cause — half the fleet would remain underutilized.

projected_state.notes: With even distribution across 8 instances, per-instance CPU should converge near the fleet average (~48%). P95 latency should drop well below the 300ms SLA threshold as no instance is saturated.

cost_impact.notes: This is a configuration change, not a capacity change — no cost delta. However, once load is balanced, the fleet-wide CPU should settle around 45-55%, which may later enable downsizing from 8 to 6 instances (~$950/mo savings). Validate post-change utilization before pursuing that optimization.

risk_assessment.primary_risk: Transient connection redistribution during the target group algorithm change may cause brief latency spikes as in-flight requests on hot instances drain.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Apply the change during a low-traffic window (weekdays 04:00-06:00 UTC based on typical API gateway patterns). Monitor per-instance CPU variance and p95 latency for 30 minutes post-change; alert if any instance exceeds 75% CPU or p95 exceeds 300ms.
risk_assessment.rollback: Revert `load_balancing_algorithm_type` to `round_robin` in Terraform and apply. The change is instantaneous on the ALB and does not require instance restarts.
risk_assessment.notes: least_outstanding_requests is well-supported on AWS ALBs and is the recommended algorithm for workloads with variable request durations.
